message:
Add snapshot tests for content scenes (json, markdown, syntax)

- Add json_scene snapshot
- Add markdown_scene snapshot
- Add syntax_scene snapshot
- Update snapshot test file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -178,3 +178,101 @@ fn snapshot_scene_list() {
     let normalized = normalize_for_snapshot(&result.stdout);
     insta::assert_snapshot!("scene_list", normalized);
 }
+
+// ============================================================================
+// Content Scene Snapshots (JSON, Markdown, Syntax)
+// ============================================================================
+
+/// Snapshot for JSON scene with feature enabled.
+///
+/// This test captures the JSON deep-dive output including:
+/// - API request/response payloads
+/// - Custom theme demonstration
+/// - Pretty-printed JSON with syntax highlighting
+#[test]
+#[cfg(feature = "showcase")]
+fn snapshot_json_scene() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--scene")
+        .arg("json")
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("42")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .timeout_secs(15)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+    assert_no_timeout(&result);
+
+    let normalized = normalize_for_snapshot(&result.stdout);
+    insta::assert_snapshot!("json_scene", normalized);
+}
+
+/// Snapshot for Markdown scene with feature enabled.
+///
+/// This test captures the Markdown deep-dive output including:
+/// - Release notes with headings, lists, code fences
+/// - Runbook excerpt with deployment instructions
+/// - CommonMark + GFM rendering
+#[test]
+#[cfg(feature = "showcase")]
+fn snapshot_markdown_scene() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--scene")
+        .arg("markdown")
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("42")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .timeout_secs(15)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+    assert_no_timeout(&result);
+
+    let normalized = normalize_for_snapshot(&result.stdout);
+    insta::assert_snapshot!("markdown_scene", normalized);
+}
+
+/// Snapshot for Syntax scene with feature enabled.
+///
+/// This test captures the Syntax deep-dive output including:
+/// - TOML config with line numbers
+/// - YAML CI/CD pipeline
+/// - Rust code snippet
+/// - Theme comparison demo
+#[test]
+#[cfg(feature = "showcase")]
+fn snapshot_syntax_scene() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--scene")
+        .arg("syntax")
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("42")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .timeout_secs(15)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+    assert_no_timeout(&result);
+
+    let normalized = normalize_for_snapshot(&result.stdout);
+    insta::assert_snapshot!("syntax_scene", normalized);
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,82 @@
+---
+source: tests/demo_showcase_snapshots.rs
+assertion_line: 214
+expression: normalized
+---
+JSON: API Payload Visualization
+
+Json renderable provides pretty-printed, syntax-highlighted JSON output.
+
+API Request: Deploy Service
+
+POST /api/v1/deployments
+
+{
+  "action": "deploy",
+  "config": {
+    "cpu_limit": "500m",
+    "health_check": {
+      "interval_seconds": 30,
+      "path": "/health",
+      "timeout_seconds": 5
+    },
+    "memory_limit": "512Mi",
+    "replicas": 3
+  },
+  "dry_run": false,
+  "environment": "production",
+  "notify": [
+    "ops@example.com",
+    "dev@example.com"
+  ],
+  "rollout": {
+    "max_surge": 1,
+    "max_unavailable": 1,
+    "strategy": "rolling"
+  },
+  "service": "nebula-api",
+  "version": "2.4.1"
+}
+
+API Response: Deployment Status
+
+200 OK
+
+{
+  "deployment_id": "dep-7f3a9b2c",
+  "details": {
+    "endpoints": [
+      "https://nebula-api.prod.example.com",
+      "https://nebula-api-internal.prod.example.com"
+    ],
+    "health_status": "healthy",
+    "replicas_ready": 3,
+    "replicas_total": 3
+  },
+  "metrics": {
+    "deploy_duration_ms": 2847,
+    "health_check_passed": true,
+    "rollout_waves": 2
+  },
+  "previous_version": "2.4.0",
+  "rollback_available": true,
+  "service": "nebula-api",
+  "status": "success",
+  "timestamp": "2026-01-27T14:32:18Z",
+  "version": "2.4.1"
+}
+
+Custom Theme
+
+Customize colors for different JSON elements:
+
+{
+  "active": true,
+  "count": 42,
+  "error": null,
+  "name": "test"
+}
+
+Default theme: keys, strings, numbers, booleans, null
+
+Json automatically highlights keys, strings, numbers, booleans, and null values.

@@ -0,0 +1,88 @@
+---
+source: tests/demo_showcase_snapshots.rs
+assertion_line: 245
+expression: normalized
+---
+Markdown: Documentation Rendering
+
+Markdown renderable converts CommonMark + GFM to styled terminal output.
+
+Release Notes
+
+Nebula API v2.4.1
+
+What's New
+
+This release brings performance improvements and stability fixes for production deployments.
+
+Features
+  • Rolling deployments: Zero-downtime updates with configurable surge
+  • Health check improvements: Faster failure detection with exponential backoff
+  • Metrics endpoint: New  /metrics  endpoint for Prometheus integration
+
+
+Breaking Changes
+│ Note: The  --legacy-mode  flag has been removed. See migration guide.
+
+Bug Fixes
+  1. Fixed memory leak in connection pool under high load
+  2. Resolved race condition in deployment rollback
+  3. Corrected timezone handling in audit logs
+
+
+Upgrade Instructions
+
+Update your deployment config:
+  [deployment]
+  version = "2.4.1"
+  strategy = "rolling"
+
+
+
+For more details, see the migration guide (https://docs.nebula.io/migrate).
+
+
+Deployment Runbook (Excerpt)
+
+Pre-Deployment Checklist
+
+Before deploying to production, verify:
+  • [ ] All tests pass in CI
+  • [ ] Staging environment validated
+  • [ ] Database migrations reviewed
+  • [ ] Rollback plan documented
+
+
+Deployment Steps
+
+1. Notify the Team
+
+Post in  #deployments :
+│ Starting production deployment of nebula-api v2.4.1
+
+2. Execute Deployment
+
+Run the deployment command:
+  kubectl apply -f k8s/production/
+  kubectl rollout status deployment/nebula-api
+
+
+
+3. Verify Health
+
+Check the health endpoint:
+  curl -s https://api.nebula.io/health | jq .
+
+
+
+Expected response:
+  {"status": "healthy", "version": "2.4.1"}
+
+
+────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+
+Last updated: January 2026
+
+
+Markdown supports headings, lists, code blocks, blockquotes, and inline formatting.

@@ -0,0 +1,98 @@
+---
+source: tests/demo_showcase_snapshots.rs
+assertion_line: 277
+expression: normalized
+---
+Syntax: Code Highlighting
+
+Syntax renderable provides highlighting for 100+ languages with configurable themes.
+
+Deployment Config (TOML)
+
+ 1 │ [deployment]
+ 2 │ name = "nebula-api"
+ 3 │ version = "2.4.1"
+ 4 │ environment = "production"
+ 5 │
+ 6 │ [deployment.resources]
+ 7 │ replicas = 3
+ 8 │ memory = "512Mi"
+ 9 │ cpu = "500m"
+10 │
+11 │ [deployment.health_check]
+12 │ path = "/health"
+13 │ interval = "30s"
+14 │ timeout = "5s"
+15 │
+16 │ [deployment.rollout]
+17 │ strategy = "rolling"
+18 │ max_unavailable = 1
+19 │ max_surge = 1
+
+CI/CD Pipeline (YAML)
+
+ 1 │ name: deploy-nebula
+ 2 │ on:
+ 3 │   push:
+ 4 │     branches: [main]
+ 5 │
+ 6 │ jobs:
+ 7 │   deploy:
+ 8 │     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+ 9 │     steps:
+10 │       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+11 │       - name: Build container
+12 │         run: docker build -t nebula-api .
+13 │       - name: Run tests
+14 │         run: cargo test --release
+15 │       - name: Deploy to production
+16 │         if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
+17 │         run: |
+18 │           kubectl apply -f k8s/
+19 │           kubectl rollout status deployment/nebula-api
+
+Request Handler (Rust)
+
+42 │ /// Handle deployment requests
+43 │ async fn deploy_handler(
+44 │     State(ctx): State<AppContext>,
+45 │     Json(req): Json<DeployRequest>,
+46 │ ) -> Result<Json<DeployResponse>, ApiError> {
+47 │     // Validate the deployment config
+48 │     req.validate()?;
+49 │
+50 │     // Start the deployment
+51 │     let deployment = ctx.deployer
+52 │         .deploy(&req.service, &req.version)
+53 │         .await?;
+54 │
+55 │     // Return the deployment status
+56 │     Ok(Json(DeployResponse {
+57 │         status: "success".into(),
+58 │         deployment_id: deployment.id,
+59 │         timestamp: Utc::now(),
+60 │     }))
+61 │ }
+Lines 42-60 of src/handlers/deploy.rs
+
+Theme Comparison
+
+The same code with different themes:
+
+Base16 Ocean (Dark):
+fn greet(name: &str) -> String {
+    format!("Hello, {}!", name)
+}
+
+Solarized Dark:
+fn greet(name: &str) -> String {
+    format!("Hello, {}!", name)
+}
+
+Inspired GitHub:
+fn greet(name: &str) -> String {
+    format!("Hello, {}!", name)
+}
+
+
+Use .theme() to select from available themes, .line_numbers(true) to show line numbers.
